title: rhus trilobata var. anisophylla
intro: Rhus trilobata var. anisophylla, also known as Desert sumac, is a variety of shrub that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues, and have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties. Desert sumac can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
